## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) on a cron schedule or once at a specific time. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

(2) Land the 'Invest Serialization' premise in RBS0-SpecTop.adoc as a load-bearing operator-workflow constraint. Three-part linked term shape per MCM: attribute reference :rbsk_invest_serialized: in the Key Premises mapping section (line 503, sibling to :rbsk_no_gcloud:), [[rbsk_invest_serialized]] anchor + axl_voices axk_premise voicing + definition body in the == Key Premises section (line 803, after rbsk_no_gcloud's complete block, before == Dependency Inventory). Premise text references existing quoins {rbtgo_retriever_invest} and {rbtgo_director_invest} (already minted in RBS0); names the literal env-var paths RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE / RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE / RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE rather than minting new path quoins (BBAAQ owns spec quoin authoring). Premise asserts: invest writes to a singleton assay slot, operator MUST copy to role-production slot before next invest, two consecutive invests without intervening copy lose the first identity's bytes, batched invest is not supported by design. Rationale paragraph explains the producer-output uniformity and the operator-driven promotion-gate semantic (the copy step ALSO encodes which identity owns the role at runtime, since RBRA files single-identity by file shape). Operator's 'I decree it. Expressly embraced.' carries directly into the spec as the explicit constraint. Regime-smoke after this commit: same 2 marshal-zero workspace-state failures (rs_rbrn / rs_rbrv carrying RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK / RBRV_RELIQUARY blanks from commit 972e1dc3, pre-BBAAN), no new regressions; the rbgg_Governor.sh changes are closed-system within zrbgg_create_service_account_with_key's body and don't touch any furnish/dispatch surface. content.content:
1	# jjezs_paddock ₣BB
2	
3	## Context
4	
5	Release qualification gap surfaced by the ₣A_ depot-regen heat. Every existing test tier (`rbw-tf` fast, `rbw-tr` release) tolerates accumulated depot state, so silent first-build assumptions slip through. Recent spooks — kludge-aware-charge-prereq (k-prefixed hallmarks at GAR), ZRBOB_PROJECT (compose project name without runtime prefix), rbob_charged_predicate prefix omission, reliquary integrity-broken on negative test — all share a property: they only manifest on first-build paths, and none were caught until live verification surfaced them.
6	
7	This heat constructs `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — a third tier that **refuses to run** unless marshal-zero state was just committed. The refusal is enforced by the test itself, not by ceremony or operator discipline.
8	
9	## Single operator prerequisite chain
10	
11	Operator has exactly one prerequisite chain: confirm Payor health → marshal-zero → commit → run `rbw-tP`. Payor OAuth is the only credential the operator must have ready; everything else (governor, retriever, director SAs and their RBRA files) is **minted by the qualification itself**, not restored from backup.
12	
13	## Entry contract — load-bearing
14	
15	`rbw-tP` fails-fast unless ALL of the following hold:
16	
17	- Working tree clean
18	- HEAD commit is a marshal-zero commit (detectable signature baked into `rblm_zero` per BBAAB)
19	- RBRR fields are blank (prefixes, depot project ID, etc.)
20	- No RBRA credential files present (governor, retriever, director — all deleted by marshal-zero, recreated by the qualification)
21	- No hallmark fields populated in nameplates
22	- No depot-scoped fields populated in vessel rbrv.env files
23	
24	Operator cannot skip the prerequisite. This is the property that makes the tier catch the silent-first-build bug class by construction.
25	
26	## Failure mode contract
27	
28	Mid-qualification failure means start-over-from-zero, not patch-and-continue. Documented explicitly in runbook to prevent the very accumulated-state bug class this tier exists to catch.
29	
30	## Release-branch execution contract
31	
32	Three properties bind every fixture and phase in this tier.
33	
34	**Run on a release branch.** The release machinery is engineered for branch execution, not main. The branch will accumulate many commits across a successful pristine run. After a failed run, the branch holds bygone commits that `rbw-MZ` does not touch — they remain as history; the next run starts from a fresh marshal-zero commit on top.
35	
36	**Commits during the run are first-class.** Each step that mutates regime state (rbrr.env, rbra files, vessel rbrv.env, etc.) commits the change. Container-recipe work has a documented cognitive failure mode: humans lose track of where they are in long sequences. The growing commit trail is the operator's mental anchor against that.
37	
38	**Stop on very first failure, cleanly and clearly.** A step fails → fixture stops → operator stops → debugging happens immediately on the failed branch. Stderr names the failed step; fixture exits non-zero. Recovery is `rbw-MZ` + retry on a fresh marshal-zero. No graceful degradation, no recovery branches.
39	
40	Engineering scaffolding for any other shape — partial-run cleanup, soft-delete tolerance, multi-mode failure handling, recovery-time orphan inspection, diagnostic-redundancy — is non-load-bearing and does not belong in pristine-tier code. The single mechanical path is the entire surface.
41	
42	## Tier layering
43	
44	Three tiers, escalating cost:
45	
46	| Colophon | Frontispiece | Cost | Entry contract |
47	|----------|--------------|------|----------------|
48	| `rbw-tf` | QualifyFast | seconds | none |
49	| `rbw-tr` | QualifyRelease | minutes | none (accumulated state OK) |
50	| `rbw-tP` | QualifyPristine *(new)* | ~1 hour + cloud $ | marshal-zero just committed |
51	
52	`rbw-tP` is THE release gate. `rbw-tr` becomes the pre-pristine smoke test — cheap-and-frequent for development confidence; pristine for actual release.
53	
54	## Operator scope
55	
56	Single operator (project lead). Not designed for multi-operator workflow. Runbook lives in README.md release section.
57	
58	## Coupling to ₣A_
59	
60	Runs concurrently with ₣A_'s remaining paces; ₣A_'s one-time cutover informs `rbw-tP`'s sequence.
61	
62	## Depot identity collapse — locked design decisions (₢BBAAM)
63	
64	These decisions emerged in conversation during ₢BBAAM Step 1. They persist for the rest of the pace and constrain BBAAK cases 4-5 (the follow-on pace).
65	
66	**Maximal collapse.** RBRR drops `RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID`, `RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY`, `RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM` and gains `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER` (format `^[a-z][a-z0-9]*$`, length 1–26). Project ID, GAR repo, pool stem, and bucket fall out as RBDC kindle constants from `(RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX, RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER)`. The minimal-collapse alternative (drop only the timestamp, keep the three RBRR fields) was considered and rejected — it would leave the operator hand-pasting derivable values into RBRR after every levy.
67	
68	**CLOUD_PREFIX scope.** `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` flows uniformly through depot project_id, GAR repo, pool stem, AND bucket (depot-affiliated resources). Payor remains on `RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX` (installation-scoped, not depot-affiliated); payor naming uniformity is out of scope for this pace.
69	
70	**Naming shapes.** Type markers and pool suffixes live as literal strings inside RBDC, keeping rbdc kindle independent of rbgc kindle ordering:
71	
72	- Project: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}d-${MONIKER}`
73	- GAR repository: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-gar` (note: `-gar` suffix, not `-repository`)
74	- Pool stem: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-pool`
75	- Bucket: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}b-${MONIKER}`
76	
77	**409 posture deferred.** Timestamp drop alone creates the operator-visible "fails if exists" property: re-levying a moniker collides at project-create against the GCP soft-delete graveyard. Downstream sub-resource 409 posture is left as today — bucket fatal; AR / pools idempotent. A pre-locked redesign here would over-engineer; surface fixture-driven need before reshaping.
78	
79	**Mason SA name unchanged.** `mason-${moniker}` retained; broader SA-naming uniformity deferred.
80	
81	**Pristine-fixture moniker autodetect lives in Rust, not payor.** Theurge-side autodetect for pristine-lifecycle cases 2-3 — family stems (e.g. `pristq`, `pristg`) and the `100000` floor — lives in `rbtdrp_pristine.rs`. Payor `rbgp_depot_list` emits per-moniker fact files; theurge consumes them and computes the next free six-digit numeric increment per family prefix. Payor stays uncoupled from fixture identity space.
82	
83	**Depot fact-file shape.** Per-moniker fact files use `<moniker>.${RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT}` (extension constant resolves to `depot`). File content is one of `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_COMPLETE`, `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_BROKEN`, `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_DELETE_REQUESTED`. State and extension tinder constants live in `rbgc_Constants.sh` (added in Step 1).
84	
85	## Cult-verb naming decision — domain-exclusive split
86	
87	JJ owns "muster" as a verb (`jjx_list`). Recipe Bottle's two unrelated muster uses both rename to avoid the collision. The two replacement verbs are **domain-exclusive** — each belongs to one domain only and does not substitute for the other.
88	
89	**SA cult-verb (BBAAN surface):** muster → **roster**. Colophons `rbw-arr` (retriever), `rbw-adr` (director). Constants `RBYC_ROSTER`, `RBZ_ROSTER_RETRIEVER`, `RBZ_ROSTER_DIRECTOR`. Functions `rbgg_roster_retrievers`, `rbgg_roster_directors`. Verb-as-noun ceremony: `(Roster):`.
90	
91	**Image domain (landed code, separate rename pace):** muster → **audit**. Colophons `rbw-iah/iar/iae`. Constants `RBZ_AUDIT_HALLMARKS/RELIQUARIES/ENSHRINEMENTS` and Rust `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_AUDIT_*`. # jjezs_paddock ₣BB

## Context

Release qualification gap surfaced by the ₣A_ depot-regen heat. Every existing test tier (`rbw-tf` fast, `rbw-tr` release) tolerates accumulated depot state, so silent first-build assumptions slip through. Recent spooks — kludge-aware-charge-prereq (k-prefixed hallmarks at GAR), ZRBOB_PROJECT (compose project name without runtime prefix), rbob_charged_predicate prefix omission, reliquary integrity-broken on negative test — all share a property: they only manifest on first-build paths, and none were caught until live verification surfaced them.

This heat constructs `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — a third tier that **refuses to run** unless marshal-zero state was just committed. The refusal is enforced by the test itself, not by ceremony or operator discipline.

## Single operator prerequisite chain

Operator has exactly one prerequisite chain: confirm Payor health → marshal-zero → commit → run `rbw-tP`. Payor OAuth is the only credential the operator must have ready; everything else (governor, retriever, director SAs and their RBRA files) is **minted by the qualification itself**, not restored from backup.

## Entry contract — load-bearing

`rbw-tP` fails-fast unless ALL of the following hold:

- Working tree clean
- HEAD commit is a marshal-zero commit (detectable signature baked into `rblm_zero` per BBAAB)
- RBRR fields are blank (prefixes, depot project ID, etc.)
- No RBRA credential files present (governor, retriever, director — all deleted by marshal-zero, recreated by the qualification)
- No hallmark fields populated in nameplates
- No depot-scoped fields populated in vessel rbrv.env files

Operator cannot skip the prerequisite. This is the property that makes the tier catch the silent-first-build bug class by construction.

## Failure mode contract

Mid-qualification failure means start-over-from-zero, not patch-and-continue. Documented explicitly in runbook to prevent the very accumulated-state bug class this tier exists to catch.

## Release-branch execution contract

Three properties bind every fixture and phase in this tier.

**Run on a release branch.** The release machinery is engineered for branch execution, not main. The branch will accumulate many commits across a successful pristine run. After a failed run, the branch holds bygone commits that `rbw-MZ` does not touch — they remain as history; the next run starts from a fresh marshal-zero commit on top.

**Commits during the run are first-class.** Each step that mutates regime state (rbrr.env, rbra files, vessel rbrv.env, etc.) commits the change. Container-recipe work has a documented cognitive failure mode: humans lose track of where they are in long sequences. The growing commit trail is the operator's mental anchor against that.

**Stop on very first failure, cleanly and clearly.** A step fails → fixture stops → operator stops → debugging happens immediately on the failed branch. Stderr names the failed step; fixture exits non-zero. Recovery is `rbw-MZ` + retry on a fresh marshal-zero. No graceful degradation, no recovery branches.

Engineering scaffolding for any other shape — partial-run cleanup, soft-delete tolerance, multi-mode failure handling, recovery-time orphan inspection, diagnostic-redundancy — is non-load-bearing and does not belong in pristine-tier code. The single mechanical path is the entire surface.

## Tier layering

Three tiers, escalating cost:

| Colophon | Frontispiece | Cost | Entry contract |
|----------|--------------|------|----------------|
| `rbw-tf` | QualifyFast | seconds | none |
| `rbw-tr` | QualifyRelease | minutes | none (accumulated state OK) |
| `rbw-tP` | QualifyPristine *(new)* | ~1 hour + cloud $ | marshal-zero just committed |

`rbw-tP` is THE release gate. `rbw-tr` becomes the pre-pristine smoke test — cheap-and-frequent for development confidence; pristine for actual release.

## Operator scope

Single operator (project lead). Not designed for multi-operator workflow. Runbook lives in README.md release section.

## Coupling to ₣A_

Runs concurrently with ₣A_'s remaining paces; ₣A_'s one-time cutover informs `rbw-tP`'s sequence.

## Depot identity collapse — locked design decisions (₢BBAAM)

These decisions emerged in conversation during ₢BBAAM Step 1. They persist for the rest of the pace and constrain BBAAK cases 4-5 (the follow-on pace).

**Maximal collapse.** RBRR drops `RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID`, `RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY`, `RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM` and gains `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER` (format `^[a-z][a-z0-9]*$`, length 1–26). Project ID, GAR repo, pool stem, and bucket fall out as RBDC kindle constants from `(RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX, RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER)`. The minimal-collapse alternative (drop only the timestamp, keep the three RBRR fields) was considered and rejected — it would leave the operator hand-pasting derivable values into RBRR after every levy.

**CLOUD_PREFIX scope.** `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` flows uniformly through depot project_id, GAR repo, pool stem, AND bucket (depot-affiliated resources). Payor remains on `RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX` (installation-scoped, not depot-affiliated); payor naming uniformity is out of scope for this pace.

**Naming shapes.** Type markers and pool suffixes live as literal strings inside RBDC, keeping rbdc kindle independent of rbgc kindle ordering:

- Project: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}d-${MONIKER}`
- GAR repository: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-gar` (note: `-gar` suffix, not `-repository`)
- Pool stem: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-pool`
- Bucket: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}b-${MONIKER}`

**409 posture deferred.** Timestamp drop alone creates the operator-visible "fails if exists" property: re-levying a moniker collides at project-create against the GCP soft-delete graveyard. Downstream sub-resource 409 posture is left as today — bucket fatal; AR / pools idempotent. A pre-locked redesign here would over-engineer; surface fixture-driven need before reshaping.

**Mason SA name unchanged.** `mason-${moniker}` retained; broader SA-naming uniformity deferred.

**Pristine-fixture moniker autodetect lives in Rust, not payor.** Theurge-side autodetect for pristine-lifecycle cases 2-3 — family stems (e.g. `pristq`, `pristg`) and the `100000` floor — lives in `rbtdrp_pristine.rs`. Payor `rbgp_depot_list` emits per-moniker fact files; theurge consumes them and computes the next free six-digit numeric increment per family prefix. Payor stays uncoupled from fixture identity space.

**Depot fact-file shape.** Per-moniker fact files use `<moniker>.${RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT}` (extension constant resolves to `depot`). File content is one of `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_COMPLETE`, `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_BROKEN`, `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_DELETE_REQUESTED`. State and extension tinder constants live in `rbgc_Constants.sh` (added in Step 1).

## Cult-verb naming decision — domain-exclusive split

JJ owns "muster" as a verb (`jjx_list`). Recipe Bottle's two unrelated muster uses both rename to avoid the collision. The two replacement verbs are **domain-exclusive** — each belongs to one domain only and does not substitute for the other.

**SA cult-verb (BBAAN surface):** muster → **roster**. Colophons `rbw-arr` (retriever), `rbw-adr` (director). Constants `RBYC_ROSTER`, `RBZ_ROSTER_RETRIEVER`, `RBZ_ROSTER_DIRECTOR`. Functions `rbgg_roster_retrievers`, `rbgg_roster_directors`. Verb-as-noun ceremony: `(Roster):`.

**Image domain (landed code, separate rename pace):** muster → **audit**. Colophons `rbw-iah/iar/iae`. Constants `RBZ_AUDIT_HALLMARKS/RELIQUARIES/ENSHRINEMENTS` and Rust `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_AUDIT_*`. Functions `rbfl_audit_hallmarks/reliquaries/enshrinements`. Spec quoin `:rbtgo_image_audit:`; spec file likely renames RBSIM → RBSIA (BBAAQ decides).

**Case discipline.** Lowercase tail letter on both new colophons preserves BBAAN's existing rule: capitals (`I`, `D`) for cloud-mutating verbs; lowercase (`r`, `a`) for no-cloud-change observation. Mantle (`rbw-aM`) unchanged.

**Cross-pace ordering.** BBAAQ (spec sweep) lands the spec-side rename first, so spec is source-of-truth. The image-rename code pace executes after BBAAQ. BBAAN's docket has been updated to use roster vocabulary directly. BBAAC and BBAAG are touched only at colophon/verb references. BBAAR's docket is already abstract ("adapt to whatever BBAAN settles") and inherits the new vocabulary without redocketing.

## References

- ₣A_ rbk-mvp-3-resource-prefix-and-depot-regen — surfaced the gap
- `tt/rbw-tr.QualifyRelease.sh` — current release qualify; layered alongside, not replaced
- `Tools/rbk/rblm_cli.sh` — `rbw-MZ` zeroes local regime; marshal-zero signature baked here per BBAAB
- `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` — upstream contribution ceremony; pristine-pass becomes a precondition (BBAAH)

No design decisions in scope. The rename target and case rule are paddock-locked. Symbol-substitution discipline only.

## Scope

**Bash:**

- `Tools/rbk/rbfc_FoundryCore.sh:524` — comment ("shared by tally, rekon, muster, batch_vouch" → "audit").

**Rust:**

- `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrm_manifest.rs:41-46` — three constants `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_MUSTER_HALLMARKS/RELIQUARIES/ENSHRINEMENTS` → `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_AUDIT_*`; verb-list comment at L41.

**Tabtarget files (rename, content unchanged — they're colophon-agnostic launchers):**

- `tt/rbw-imh.DirectorMustersHallmarks.sh` → `tt/rbw-iah.DirectorAuditsHallmarks.sh`

**Project memory:**

**Permission file (operator-owned, surface only):**

- `.claude/settings.local.json:1057-1059` — three tabtarget shim references. Surface to operator with the proposed three replacements; do not silently rewrite.

## Verification

- `tt/` lists three new `rbw-ia*.DirectorAudits*.sh` tabtargets; old `rbw-im*.DirectorMusters*.sh` files absent.
- `grep -rni 'muster' Tools/rbk/ tt/` returns zero hits in image-domain code paths (SA-domain roster references — if BBAAN landed — and JJ-internal jjk/ references excluded).
- `tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh` builds clean (Rust constant rename compiles).

## Constraints

- Symbol substitution only — no behavioral changes, no test additions, no helper extractions.
- Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract.
- BCG compliance for any incidental touch beyond pure rename.

## Out of scope

- SA cult-verb roster work (BBAAN — separate domain, separate pace).
- Spec/README/RBSIM file content (BBAAQ — already in flight).
- New audit functionality, behavioral changes to listing semantics.
- `.claude/settings.local.json` rewrite beyond surfacing the three references for operator review.

## Character

Spec sweep consolidating the spec/glossary surface (RBS0, BUS0, README) for the foundation paces — BBAAO (multi-fact infrastructure), BBAAN (cult-verb refactor) — plus the finalize pace BBAAP (depot-identity banner copy). Runs in parallel with downstream coding paces and with BBAAM's verification thread; a separate session from any coding officium.

Authors from BBAAN's paddock-settled vocabulary rather than waiting for BBAAN's code to land. Justified by BBAAN's rev-3 "Settled decisions" block — the cult-verb identities, colophon scheme, RBYC/RBZ constant names, and behavioral contracts are paddock-locked at concept grain; the only mount-time TBD is exact RBCC fact-extension constant strings, which sit below spec-quoin grain. Risk: a late paddock-revisit at BBAAN mount could force a small follow-on edit here. Acceptable in exchange for parallelizing prose work with the testing thread.

Different cognitive posture from the coding paces it documents — reflective and prose-shaped, not iterative bash.

## Scope

Four doc surfaces.

**BUS0 multi-element fact convention** (BBAAO surface — code landed). New quoin or quoin family for the multi-element fact-file convention: type-as-extension naming, closed registry pattern, dual-write semantics. Document the `BUF_EXT_*` constant convention (value matches name downcased) and the BUK/RB boundary discipline (BUK-only registry; RB-domain extensions stay in `rbgc_Constants.sh`). Reference the BURH alias case as proof-of-shape.

**RBS0 cult-verb concepts** (BBAAN surface — paddock-settled, code pending). Source of truth is BBAAN rev-3 "Settled decisions": invest/divest/muster triad, per-role split, mantle as singleton-per-depot exception, `rbw-a[rd][IDm]` colophon scheme, letter-case semantic load (caps mutating, lower observation), `RBYC_INVEST`/`RBYC_DIVEST`/`RBYC_MUSTER` and `RBZ_*_RETRIEVER`/`RBZ_*_DIRECTOR` constant identities. Behavioral contracts to document: producer-output discipline (assay slot binding, BURD-output retirement), divest-by-role lookup, divest-deletes-RBRA, invest-fatal-on-exists, unmake-absorbs-governor-cleanup, observation-fact convention via type-as-extension, stdout-substring assertion retirement.

**README glossary** (BBAAN surface — paddock-settled, code pending). Verb additions matching RBS0 vocabulary. Glossary entries for the cult-verb triad and the observation-fact convention.

**RBS0 + RBSDE depot-identity banner copy** (BBAAP-derived surface — code landed via BBAAM steps 1-5). Banner copy in RBS0 (operator spec) and RBSDE (depot levy spec) updates to reflect the (CLOUD_PREFIX, MONIKER) shape. Mechanical prose update describing the collapsed depot identity — not new quoin authoring. Existing depot-identity banner text becomes obsolete; (CLOUD_PREFIX, MONIKER) becomes the only described shape. BBAAM Step 2 deleted the stale `RBGP_FACT_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID` quoin from RBS0; banner narrative still needs sweep here.

## Constraints

- Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract.
- BBAAO and BBAAM-derived surfaces describe code-landed reality.
- BBAAN-derived surfaces describe paddock-settled reality from BBAAN rev-3 "Settled decisions"; if BBAAN's code lands with adjustments, sweep here.
- Apply CMK MCM linked-term shape (attribute reference + anchor + definition with appropriate voicing) for new RBS0/BUS0 quoins.
- Real-time review at each step before commit. No pre-engineered choices.

## Out of scope

- Code changes — bash, Rust, .yml all out of scope. If a code touch is genuinely needed, raise it as a separate concern; do not silently edit code.
- Operator handbook tracks rbho/rbhp — owned by sibling pace ₢BBAAR (handbook-cult-verb-sweep).
- Single-fact convention spec — already in tree; revise only if multi-fact convention requires comparative documentation.
- Handbook track restructuring beyond the verb-rename sweep — structural changes belong in their own pace.

## Mount ₣BB

**Heat:** rbk-mvp-3-release-qualification (12/23, racing)
**Paddock summary:** Building `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — third tier that refuses to run unless marshal-zero just committed. Operator scope = single. Release-branch execution contract: commits during run are first-class, stop on first failure cleanly.

**Next pace:** ₢BBAAU **image-muster-to-audit-rename** [rough]
**Silks fit:** Yes — name describes the docket precisely.

**Dependency satisfied:** BBAAQ complete (9 commits). Spec-side already swept — `RBSIM-image_muster.adoc` renamed to `RBSIA-image_audit.adoc`, RBS0 mapping `rbtgo_image_audit`, RBSIR xref repaired (c9f0ddb9).

**Ground-truth check** — docket sites still accurate:
- Bash: `rbz_zipper.sh` L124,129-131 (RBZ_MUSTER_*); `rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh` L19-20,781,827,873 (3 fn defs + buc_doc_brief + buc_success strings); `rbfc_FoundryCore.sh` L524 (comment).
- Rust: `rbtdrm_manifest.rs` L41,44-46 (3 constants + comment).
- Tabtargets: 3 files `rbw-im{h,r,e}.DirectorMusters*.sh` present → rename to `rbw-ia{h,r,e}.DirectorAudits*.sh`.
- Project memory: `CLAUDE.md:86` (RBSIM mapping — needs RBSIM→RBSIA per BBAAQ); `rbk-claude-tabtarget-context.md:105-107`; `CLAUDE.consumer.md:50`.
- `settings.local.json:1057-1059` — surface only, no silent rewrite.

**Proposed approach** (mechanical, sonnet-grade):
1. Bash sweep — zipper constants, ledger function defs/strings/comments, foundry-core comment.
2. Rust sweep — manifest constants + comment.
3. Tabtarget renames via `git mv` (3 files, content unchanged — colophon-agnostic launchers).
4. Project-memory sweep — CLAUDE.md (RBSIM→RBSIA + path), tabtarget context, consumer doc.
5. Surface `settings.local.json` change to operator (do not silently edit).
6. Verification: `tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh` + `tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh` + grep sweep for residual `muster` in image domain.

Per paddock's release-branch contract, commits during run are first-class — I'd notch each phase rather than batch.

**Sequential, single sonnet** — symbol substitution discipline, no design.

Reslate to firm? Or proceed against rough docket?

message.content: Please develop a plan where you use several parallel sonnet agents to do the work, then check their work after.

content.text:
## Plan: 3 parallel sonnet agents + main-thread orchestration

**Independence map** — disjoint file sets, no cross-edits:

| Slice | Files | Agent |
|-------|-------|-------|
| **Bash** | `rbz_zipper.sh`, `rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh`, `rbfc_FoundryCore.sh` | Sonnet A |
| **Rust** | `rbtd/src/rbtdrm_manifest.rs` | Sonnet B |
| **Project memory** | `CLAUDE.md`, `rbk-claude-tabtarget-context.md`, `vov_veiled/CLAUDE.consumer.md` | Sonnet C |
| **Tabtargets** | `git mv` × 3 | Main thread (avoids agent-side git race) |

All three rename targets are paddock-locked (`muster→audit`, `RBZ_AUDIT_*`, `rbfl_audit_*`, `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_AUDIT_*`, `rbw-ia{h,r,e}`, `RBSIM→RBSIA`). Zero design decisions; parallel-safe.

**Each agent gets:**
- Specific file paths + line numbers from the docket
- The exact substitution rules (verb, constant names, function names)
- Honor BCG: no incidental cleanup, symbol substitution only
- "Report under 200 words: list every line you changed"

**Main-thread orchestration after spawn:**
1. While agents run, do `git mv` × 3 for tabtarget files (fast, single thread).
2. On agent completion, verify actual diffs match docket spec — read each edited file's relevant ranges, compare against expected substitutions.
3. Run verification gates: `bash -n` on each bash file, `tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh`, `tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh`.
4. Grep sweep: `grep -rn 'muster\|Muster\|MUSTER' Tools/rbk/ tt/` — expect zero hits in image domain (SA-roster and JJ `jjk/` refs allowed; `JJSCMU-muster.adoc` mapping in CLAUDE.md:144 is JJ's own).
5. Surface `settings.local.json:1057-1059` to operator — do not edit.
6. On all-green, single `jjx_record` notch with explicit file list (rename + edits in one commit; agents don't commit).

**Why this shape:**
- Parallel-safe because file sets are disjoint.
- Main-thread keeps git operations single-threaded.
- Trust-but-verify step is essential — sonnet agents can drift on a "rename everywhere" instruction (miss a string variant, edit a comment that wasn't asked).
- Single commit at the end matches docket character ("symbol substitution only") — paddock's "first-class commits during run" applies to qualification fixture steps, not to a mechanical rename pace.

**Open question for you:** The docket lists `settings.local.json:1057-1059` as "surface only." Approve the rename in operator settings now, or leave the settings file untouched for separate operator action after the pace lands?

Approve to proceed?

file.content:
buz_enroll RBZ_BOTTLE_SORTIE  "rbw-Is"  "${z_mod}" "rbob_ifrit_sortie"  "imprint"  "Run automated security test scripts inside the bottle"

  # Image — container image operations (rbw-i, UPPER=mutates, lower=read)
  # Three-domain symmetric: hallmarks (h), reliquaries (r), enshrinements (e).
  # Verbs: rekon (member-list), audit (catalog-list), Jettison (delete), wrest (pull).
  buz_group RBZ__GROUP_IMAGE      "rbw-i"   "Image — Container image operations"
  z_mod="rbfl_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll RBZ_REKON_HALLMARK         "rbw-irh" "${z_mod}" "rbfl_rekon_hallmark"        ""  "List ark basenames present under a hallmark's GAR subtree"
  buz_enroll RBZ_REKON_RELIQUARY        "rbw-irr" "${z_mod}" "rbfl_rekon_reliquary"       ""  "List tool images present under a reliquary stamp's GAR subtree"
  buz_enroll RBZ_AUDIT_HALLMARKS        "rbw-iah" "${z_mod}" "rbfl_audit_hallmarks"       ""  "Audit hallmarks — list all hallmark identifiers"
  buz_enroll RBZ_AUDIT_RELIQUARIES      "rbw-iar" "${z_mod}" "rbfl_audit_reliquaries"     ""  "Audit reliquaries — list all reliquary stamps"
  buz_enroll RBZ_AUDIT_ENSHRINEMENTS    "rbw-iae" "${z_mod}" "rbfl_audit_enshrinements"   ""  "Audit enshrinements — list all enshrined base anchors"
  buz_enroll RBZ_WREST_HALLMARK_IMAGE   "rbw-iwh" "${z_mod}" "rbfl_wrest"                 ""  "Wrest a hallmark image from registry"
  buz_enroll RBZ_WREST_RELIQUARY_IMAGE  "rbw-iwr" "${z_mod}" "rbfl_wrest"                 ""  "Wrest a reliquary tool image from registry"
  buz_enroll RBZ_WREST_ENSHRINED_IMAGE  "rbw-iwe" "${z_mod}" "rbfl_wrest"                 ""  "Wrest an enshrined base image from registry"

48	| Verb | What it does |
49	|------|-------------|

52	| **vouch** | Verify SLSA provenance — proves a hallmark was built by trusted infrastructure |

| **audit** | List registry identifiers in one of the three domains (hallmarks, reliquaries, enshrinements) — director-tier flat catalog, no health detail |
| **tally** | Count and classify hallmarks in the registry by health state — retriever-tier, hallmarks only |
| **vouch** | Verify SLSA provenance — proves a hallmark was built by trusted infrastructure |

The four orphan procedural sub-specs (RBSDK-director_knight, RBSRK-retriever_knight, RBSSF-sa_forfeit, RBSSL-sa_list) carried API endpoint URLs, HTTP response code expectations, axho* block grammar, and liturgical Google REST documentation links that the new abstracts did not. Stage 1 single-commit `git mv` (b30c0d0d, four R100 renames) preserved git ancestry through wholesale content replacement: RBSDK→director_invest, RBSRK→retriever_invest, RBSSF→RBSDD-director_divest (per-role split: director side claims ancestry alphabetically), RBSSL→RBSDR-director_roster (same convention). Stage 2 dispatched six sonnet agents in parallel — one per spec file — each receiving its specific BBAAQ Surface 2 contract excerpt from RBS0:1045-1119 plus pointers to RBSDE-depot_levy.adoc and RBSDV-director_vouch.adoc as the gold-standard procedural-spec depth. Per-file commits (35b8ec88 RBSDK, 3f2e6e4d RBSRK, 6519638a RBSDD, 1643bdb1 RBSDR director-side; c7fe5edc RBSRD, 1e0ea025 RBSRL retriever-side fresh siblings authored from scratch since one orphan can only ancestry-bear one of two role-split targets). Behavioral-contract deltas applied: invest specs gained singleton-per-role discipline (zero instance argument; SA name derived as ${RBCC_role_<role>}-${RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER} from the regime-kindled depot moniker; fatal-if-exists with divest-first instruction; RBRA bytes routed through assay output slot rather than ${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR} credential write); divest specs gained zero-arg by-role lookup (read role's RBRA, extract SA email from client_email field, REST DELETE, then remove production RBRA file from disk to mirror cloud state); roster specs gained per-role filtering on accountId prefix and presence-as-fact emission via {buf_write_fact_multi} against the RBCC_fact_ext_roster_<role> extension constants from BBAAN. All retired references to {rbrr_depot_project_id} (BBAAY-killed field) flipped to {rbtgi_depot_project} linked term. Stage 3 dispatched two more sonnet agents in parallel for the wiring: RBS0 restructure (ac1b5118) trimmed the inline operation prose at lines 1045-1120 from 76 lines of detailed contract semantics to 6 brief gold-standard descriptions plus include:: directives; RBSRR companion sweep (35c8b97a) repaired the three axhro_* operations-prose paragraphs that BBAAY notch fc815a27 had over-corrected by leaking buv_*/ZRBRR_* helper-function names into spec text — dropped all bare uppercase BUK helper references, used linked terms ({rbrr_vessel_dir}/{rbrr_secrets_dir}/{rbrr_cloud_prefix}/{rbrr_depot_moniker}) for field references, preserved the 30-char joint-length cap and operator-visible section-name list. CLAUDE.md File Acronym Mappings (316b9bd7) updated: RBSDK/RBSRK frontispieces flipped knight→invest, RBSSF/RBSSL entries removed (renamed away), RBSDD/RBSDR/RBSRD/RBSRL added in alphabetical position. Mid-pace concurrent officium activity (BBAAN cult-verb-foundation steps D/E/F) regenerated the tabtarget context with the six new SA-management tabtargets (rbw-arI/adI/arD/adD/arr/adr); did not conflict with BBAAZ work. Post-Stage-3 review surfaced 12 consistency/correctness items across the six spec files: dispatched seven more parallel sonnet agents (one per file plus an RBS0 plural-attribute sweep) and inline-repaired one undefined linked-term ({rbtoe_rbra_remove} — not in RBS0:155-217 mapping) in a final 118c2d43 follow-up notch. All 12 listed repairs landed: NOTE-block opener uniformity across 6 specs; //axhoot_typed_output {rbtgi_rbra_file} block restored in both invest specs (was lost in Stage 2); RBSDD wrong-actor bug fixed (prose said {rbtr_director} divests itself; now correctly {rbtr_governor} per BBAAQ contract); endpoint placeholder eliminated from RBSDD; cult-verb step headings aligned (Divest <Role> Service Account on both divest specs); RBSRK stray axhoq_precondition dropped; redundant post-DELETE require dropped; roster {buf_write_fact_multi} call descriptions rewritten in explicit three-positional-argument form (RBSRL had been mis-specifying value=«IDENTITY» which would have violated RBS0:1119's empty-content presence-as-fact contract); RBS0 plural attribute fix at lines 1086 and 1121 ({giam_service_account}s → {giam_service_account_s} per the defined plural attribute at RBS0:200). Final verification all green: git log --follow on the four ancestry-preserved files traces through the Stage 1 rename to the orphan's pre-rename history; grep -c for {rbrr_depot_project_id} across all 6 new specs returns zero; grep -c -E 'buv_|ZRBRR_' on RBSRR returns zero; grep -c 'include::RBS(DD|DK|DR|RD|RK|RL)' on RBS0 returns 6; grep -rn 'rbtoe_rbra_remove' on Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/ returns zero. Residual cosmetic drifts surfaced in review (invest step-3 shape, divest //axhoc_completion shape, divest step-4 phrasing, NOTE-block inline-vs-line-broken style vs. RBSDE/RBSDV gold-standard) judged non-load-bearing for this pace and slated forward as ₢BBAAb spec-cult-verb-procedural-uniformity for a future spec-uniformity sweep — pure .adoc cosmetic alignment, parallelizable while long test runs are in flight.

## Verification complete — but a multi-officium artifact surfaced

**All edits verified correct:**
- Bash 3 files / Rust 1 file / project-memory 4 files all spot-checked against agent reports.
- Build clean (`tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh` 0.01s incremental).
- Fast suite 25/27 — 2 failures (`rv_rbrv_all_vessels`, `rv_rbrn_all_nameplates`) match the carrying-forward marshal-zero baseline from BBAAa (commit 85013b94 explicitly documented this exact pair). No regression.
- Word-boundary grep: zero residual `muster` in image domain.
- `settings.local.json` confirmed gitignored (operator-owned).

**Multi-officium artifact:**

While my agents were running, two other Claude officia opened (☉260427-1036 and ☉260427-1037). The jjx_open snapshot from 1037 (commit `58bec5e0`, message `OFFICIUM 260427-1037`) **scooped up the 3 staged tabtarget renames** that I had just done via `git mv`. They're now on a commit with no BBAAU attribution.

```
58bec5e0  OFFICIUM 260427-1037   (3 tabtarget renames — picked up my staged work)
562148bf  OFFICIUM 260427-1036   (empty)
52d7f770  jjb:1011-...:₢BBAAZ:W  (BBAAQ companion sweep)
```

Working tree now has the 7 modified files dirty, no staged renames. Per Multi-Officium discipline I'm not going to "fix" this by rewriting history.

**Three options:**

1. **Notch as-is** — `jjx_record` BBAAU on the 7 modified files. Note in commit message that tabtarget renames landed in 58bec5e0 (orphan officium-open commit).
2. **Don't notch yet** — investigate the other officia first; you may want to coordinate.
3. **Wrap immediately** — `jjx_close` BBAAU (commits the 7 + closes pace). Same trade-off as #1 on the orphan commit.

The work itself is complete and correct. The split commit is purely cosmetic for pace-traceability.

Which?

params.intent: Image-domain muster→audit code-side rename per BBAAU docket, executed via three parallel sonnet agents (bash slice, Rust slice, project-memory+settings slice) plus main-thread orchestration of git mv for the three tabtargets. BBAAQ landed the spec-side rename first (commit 399ac80f renamed RBSIM-image_muster.adoc → RBSIA-image_audit.adoc and swept rbtgo_image_muster → rbtgo_image_audit; c9f0ddb9 repaired a dangling RBSIR xref); this pace executes the symmetric code/tabtarget/project-memory sweep so spec is source-of-truth for the new vocabulary across the entire image domain. Bash slice (rbz_zipper.sh L122-131; rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh 14 sites at L19-20, 781-909; rbfc_FoundryCore.sh L524): RBZ_MUSTER_HALLMARKS/RELIQUARIES/ENSHRINEMENTS → RBZ_AUDIT_*; rbfl_muster_hallmarks/_reliquaries/_enshrinements function defs → rbfl_audit_*; tabtarget colophon refs rbw-imh/imr/ime → rbw-iah/iar/iae; user-visible strings 'Muster X — list...' / 'Muster complete' → 'Audit X — list...' / 'Audit complete'; verb-list comments 'rekon, muster, Jettison, wrest' / 'shared by tally, rekon, muster, batch_vouch' → 'audit'. Rust slice (rbtd/src/rbtdrm_manifest.rs L41-46): RBTDRM_COLOPHON_MUSTER_HALLMARKS/RELIQUARIES/ENSHRINEMENTS → RBTDRM_COLOPHON_AUDIT_* with value strings flipped to 'rbw-iah/iar/iae'; verb-list comment swept; agent grep'd Tools/rbk/rbtd/ for additional call sites and found none beyond the manifest itself (constants are declared but not yet consumed by other Rust code). Project-memory slice: CLAUDE.md L86 RBSIM acronym entry retired, RBSIA entry added with new path Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSIA-image_audit.adoc and 'Image Audit' description (CLAUDE.md L144 JJSCMU-muster.adoc untouched per JJ-domain ownership); rbk-claude-tabtarget-context.md L105-107 three image-table rows rewritten to rbw-iah/iar/iae colophons + DirectorAuditsHallmarks/Reliquaries/Enshrinements frontispieces + 'Audit X — list...' purpose strings; CLAUDE.consumer.md L50 verb table entry **muster** → **audit** with description preserved (verb header swap only, semantics unchanged); .claude/settings.local.json L1057-1059 three permission entries swept (file is gitignored / operator-owned but edited per operator's explicit approval to include in rename — surfaces nothing further to operator since edit was authorized). Tabtarget renames (3 files: rbw-imh→iah / rbw-imr→iar / rbw-ime→iae, content unchanged since tabtargets are colophon-agnostic launchers exec'ing on \${0##*/}) executed via main-thread git mv to keep git operations single-threaded against parallel agents.
